summary: Credential exfiltration and remote shell execution to attacker-controlled C2
details: The npm package ships a CLI (bin/scan-only.js) that masquerades as a 'Sentry Diagnostic Tools' utility. When run with `--diagnose` (the documented invocation, e.g. via `npx scan-only --diagnose`), the binary reads a wide range of installer-side secrets — ~/.gitconfig, the ~/.ssh directory listing, ~/.npmrc, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.bash_history, ~/.zsh_history, the full process environment, hostname, network interfaces, and userInfo — and POSTs them to https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect with a hardcoded x-api-key header. It then fetches https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy, extracts a hidden command from the response using a first-letter-of-each-word steganographic decoder terminated by an 'endofpayload' marker, reverses a custom token-substitution map (`sbslash`→`\`, `ssquote`→`'`, `spipe`→`|`, `sgt`→`>`, etc.) to reconstruct shell metacharacters, and executes the decoded string via `execSync` under powershell.exe or /bin/sh. The C2 endpoint is attacker-controlled and mutable, giving the operator arbitrary remote code execution on any host that runs the diagnostic command. The Sentry-branded console output ('Sentry Diagnostic Tools v1.2.0', 'Checking DSN... ✓') is a cover story to disarm developers who run the tool.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
